ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-01-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Bassi appeared by zoom for Claire Yeaton-Risley (Petitioner).

Mr. Bassi explained circumstances and services provided to support the request for additional fees for time spent with the American Red Cross on the 2018 petition for final distribution that did not get filed. Mr. Bassi also addressed the request for extraordinary fees stating that it would have been inappropriate for a Paralegal to make a determination with regard to the missing attachments. In addition, Mr. Bassi stated that he has no comment on the lack of time records for Ms. Risley's request for additional compensation and that this is the first time he has had someone ask for them. The Petition for Final Distribution is under submission. The Court informed Mr. Bassi that it anticipates some small reductions.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-21-2020 Continued To Aug-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christy Styer for Claire Yeaton-Risley (Administrator).

Ms. Styer provided a courtesy copy of the report and a proposed order. Ms. Styer stated that what is holding this up is the litigation in Hawaii, that it is the last element in getting this estate closed, that there have been numerous delays in Hawaii and that a settlement agreement was executed one year ago. Ms. Styer also stated that there is an issue with the title company issuing a title insurance policy on a quit claim deed, that the pandemic closed the title company down for a while, that the title company is now getting out from under and that the Beneficiary does not want an assignment of the litigation. In addition, Ms. Styer stated that half of the estate was already distributed and that they are waiting on final payment on the land sale. The Fourth Account and Report of Conservator, etc. is continued to August 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Loren Lopin for Claire Yeaton-Risley. Stacie Nelson for The American Red Cross.

The Court stated that a couple of things have been filed, that one is in the form of a 3rd amended petition, that subsequently a first supplement to the 2nd amended petition was filed, that it is concerned that the record is confusing and that it can be cleared up by the administrator filing a withdrawal to the 5/17/16 filing. Mr. Lopin stated that he will do that. The Court stated that we now have an original will and it can be handled on the basis of the 2nd amended petition, that it is inclined to take the second amended petition for probate of lost will and letters testamentary under submission upon receipt of the withdrawal of the 5/17/16 filing. The Court further stated that the withdrawal must be filed by 6/1/16.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
